location: Guaranteed drop from defeating the boss Knight of the Solitary Gaol (this boss was renamed from its original launch name, 'Blackgaol Knight' — both names refer to the same fight), found in the undercroft of the Western Nameless Mausoleum.
tactics: From the Scorched Ruins Site of Grace, head west past withered trees and large gravestones; the mausoleum entrance is marked by two lit braziers, guarded by nearby Blue Worm enemies.

The boss itself wields this exact greatsword one-handed alongside a repeating crossbow in the other, alternating between an up-to-10-shot arrow barrage at range and heavy overhead/spinning slashes up close — listen for its reload sound to time dodges.

It's weak to Lightning damage, is parryable and backstabbable, and drinks a healing flask mid-fight that can be interrupted with a stagger; leveling Scadutree Blessing before the attempt is strongly recommended, as it hits very hard early.
There is no Site of Grace at the mausoleum itself, only a Stake of Marika and a summoning pool at the entrance for respawning/co-op.
lore: Forged for the Knights of the Solitary Gaol, who are said to have surrendered their own hearts through a secret rite in exchange for greater battle prowess. Each blade carries two engraved crests — one for the light of birth, the other for solitude.

builds.name: Pure Strength heavy-hitter
builds.why: B-grade Strength scaling with a 27 STR requirement (18 two-handed) makes it a top-tier pick for pure Strength builds, and its guard stats rival colossal weapons despite being a two-handed sword.

builds.name: Strength/Dex hybrid tank
builds.why: A minor Dex scaling on top of its Strength base, paired with the highest guard boost of any greatsword, suits a hybrid build that wants to block confidently while still swinging a real damage weapon.
